title: How much do you need to retire at 40?
subtitle: A detailed breakdown of the math behind early retirement
description: See how much money you need to retire at 40 using annual expenses, safe withdrawal rates, bridge accounts, and realistic savings targets.

faqs.question: How much money do you need to retire at 40?
faqs.answer: A common target for retiring at 40 is 30 to 33 times annual expenses, because the portfolio may need to last 50 years or more.

faqs.question: Is the 4% rule safe for retiring at 40?
faqs.answer: The 4% rule is often too aggressive for retiring at 40 because it was designed around a traditional 30-year retirement, not a 50- to 60-year horizon.

faqs.question: What withdrawal rate should a 40-year-old retiree consider?
faqs.answer: Many early retirees planning to stop work at 40 use a more conservative withdrawal rate around 3.0% to 3.5%.
